articles.title: AH Vishwanath announces retirement from electoral politics
articles.description: Mysuru: Former minister and BJP MLC AH Vishwanath on Thursday announced his retirement from electoral politics. He said that he will not contest any e.

articles.title: Skyroot hits unicorn status, but India's private space race needs clearer skies
articles.description: Hyderabad's Skyroot Aerospace has become India's first space launch vehicle unicorn. This achievement fuels the nation's space technology sector. The company is preparing for its Vikram-1 rocket launch. This development signifies a major shift for Indian businesses. The space sector presents a significant economic opportunity. However, government decisions regarding technology transfer could impact private companies like Skyroot and Agnikul Cosmos.

articles.title: Belagavi police warn against social media misuse by rowdy-sheeters via minors
articles.description: Belagavi: The Belagavi district police have taken serious note of the growing misuse of social media by rowdy-sheeters who are allegedly using minors .

articles.title: David Attenborough turns 100: The man who made the World fall in love with nature
articles.description: Sir David Attenborough, a renowned naturalist, has spent a century fostering understanding between humans and the wild. His work highlights the deep connection possible with animals, moving beyond mere observation. Attenborough championed evolution, revealing the beauty in life's journey. He transformed nature documentaries, always prioritizing the natural world over himself.

articles.title: Why TVK chief Vijay has to wait to become Tamil Nadu Chief Minister | Tell Me Why | Episode 41
articles.description: Tamil Nadu politics has taken a dramatic turn after actor-turned-politician Vijay’s TVK emerged as the single largest party, defeating both DMK and AIADMK. But why is there still no Chief Minister? The controversy now centers on Governor Rajendra Arlekar, who has refused to invite Vijay to form the government without proof of majority support. Constitutional experts argue the Governor must first invite the single largest party, making this a major debate over democracy, constitutional norms, and political strategy. Is this constitutional caution or political interference? Here’s a full breakdown of the unfolding Tamil Nadu power struggle.

articles.title: ‘God Is Good’: Karoline Leavitt Announces Arrival Of Baby Viviana
articles.description: ‘God Is Good’: Karoline Leavitt Announces Arrival Of Baby Viviana

articles.title: Bengal Man Took 'No Haircut' Vow Till Trinamool Lost. He's Now Shaved His Head
articles.description: Baneshwar Barman, a resident of Sitalkuchi's Baman Para village, finally cut his hair this week.

articles.title: Andhra Pradesh conducts Census in Kotia villages, reigniting Odisha border dispute
articles.description: Andhra Pradesh has reportedly commenced its Census in Kotia villages, reigniting the long-standing border dispute with Odisha. This move follows Odisha's recent administrative visit and Census completion. Both states claim control over these villages, a matter pending before the Supreme Court since 1968, intensifying tensions in the region.

articles.title: Thimarody’s bail plea rejected
articles.description: Mangaluru: Sixth additional district & sessions judge, Rajesh Maruthi Kamate, Thursday rejected the bail application filed by activist Mahesh Shet.

articles.title: African proverb of the day: 'No matter how beautiful and well crafted a coffin might look, it will not...' – why no one can escape death and lessons on why no luxury, wealth and outward beauty can hide pain, suffering and sorrow
articles.description: African proverb of the day: An African proverb states that no matter how beautiful a coffin appears, it won't make anyone desire death. This highlights the human instinct to value life and seek meaning, even amidst hardship. The proverb reminds us to look beyond superficial appearances and recognize the inherent preciousness of life.

articles.title: 'We will not do BPL for the sake of BPL' - Tamim calls for reset
articles.description: The BCB interim head also called for accountability, insisting the league's long-term future depends on financially credible and professionally run franchises

articles.title: Woman fired because husband worked at rival firm, takes employer to court and wins Rs 95 lakh
articles.description: A Shanghai woman was fired simply because her husband worked at a rival company. The court ruled the dismissal was unlawful and ordered the firm to pay her Rs 95 lakh.

articles.title: Karnataka: HC extends stay on FIR against MLA, election officials till June 3
articles.description: The Bengaluru High Court has extended a stay on an FIR concerning the Sringeri vote recount. The case involves BJP MLA DN Jeevaraj and election officials KN Ramesh and Vedamurthy. The FIR was filed following a complaint about alleged electoral malpractice. The court's decision provides temporary relief to the accused. The Congress party plans to challenge the revised election results.

articles.title: ‘In-house enemies:’ Ahead of Trump visit, China sentences two defence ministers to death!
articles.description: Virendra Pandit New Delhi: Ahead of US President Donald Trump’s scheduled visit next week to China, Beijing has sentenced two former defence ministers to ‘suspended’ death sentences on alleged corruption charges, the media reported on Thursday. A military court on Thursday sentenced Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu to death with a two-year reprieve. This [...]

articles.description: Actor Riva Arora recalls how a Blinkit delivery agent allegedly verbally harassed her and her family for nearly 40 minutes, prompting them to call the Mumbai Police.

articles.description: The cabinet of Samrat Chaudhary was expanded on Thursday. The JD(U) inducted three new leaders into the cabinet, including Nishant Kumar, Bulo Mandal, and Shweta Gupta.

articles.title: Global markets: AI and chip boom fuel expansion of trillion-dollar club
articles.description: Currently, 13 companies worldwide are valued at more than $1 trillion. Of these, 10 are from the US, with Taiwan, South Korea, and Saudi Arabia contributing one each. An additional 13 companies globally sit at the half-trillion-dollar mark, nine of them American, and one each from China, the Netherlands, South Korea, and Mexico.

articles.title: Puducherry Assembly Election Results 2026 at a glance: Vote share, seats tally and more
articles.description: The NDA alliance won the Puducherry Assembly elections on May 4, with the incumbent AINRC coming back to power with the help of its allies. Here are graphics giving an overview of the results.

articles.description: At a Mumbai meeting, two BJP leaders stressed that party workers should use the SIR to take its anti-Bangladeshi infiltration drive to its logical end.

articles.title: Vendors accuse Bhooth Bangla makers of Rs 30 lakh non-payment; Balaji denies allegations
articles.description: Bhooth Bangla has landed in controversy after vendors accused the makers of unpaid dues, including an alleged pending payment of Rs 30 lakh. The film continues to perform strongly at the ticket window.

articles.title: ‘While I do shed crocodile tears, I was left with no other option’, says Mehli Mistry on vote against Srinivasan
articles.description: Mehli Mistry defended his decision to oppose trustee reappointments ahead of the Tata Sons board meeting discussing governance and representation issues.

articles.title: “We Will Outlast Them”: Prof. Marandi Warns U.S. Of A Long War; Slams UAE For Aiding Ground Invasion
articles.description: From the heart of Tehran, India Today speaks with Professor Mohammad Marandi on the escalating 39-day-old conflict. Marandi asserts that despite heavy damage to Iranian petrochemical and pharmaceutical infrastructure, the leadership remains unified. He characterizes the U.S. "maximum pressure" and naval blockade as a global economic catastrophe that Iran is prepared to outwait. Marandi specifically warns neighboring regimes, like the UAE, that hosting U.S. ground troops and drones makes them direct participants in the war. Defiant and resolute, he emphasises that for Iran, this is a "fight for survival" that they are destined to win.

articles.title: 9 terror camps, 11 airfields, 13 aircraft: India lists Pakistan losses on Op Sindoor anniversary
articles.description: When asked about losses on the Indian side, Air Marshal AK Bharti said, “Neither has any of our military infrastructure been damaged, nor civilian structure.”

articles.title: Questioning religious practices will break religion and civilisation: Supreme Court in Dawoodi Bohras case
articles.description: Supreme Court warns that questioning religious practices could disrupt religion and civilization, amid hearings on women's discrimination in faith.

articles.title: Many MLAs with Venugopal, party workers back Satheesan: In race for Kerala CM post, AICC assesses options
articles.description: Ramesh Chennithala is another leader in contention, and his camp is confident his seniority will be taken into account.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Marcus Aurelius: ‘Never let the future disturb you; you will meet it, if you...’ - Why worrying too much about the future steals peace from the present, explained by the Stoic philosopher
articles.description: Marcus Aurelius Quote of the Day suggests that fear of the future often creates more suffering than the actual challenges themselves. In his famous words from Meditations, the Stoic philosopher reminds people that the same reason, strength, and resilience helping them survive todays struggles will also help them face tomorrows uncertainties. It encourages people to stop overthinking the future and focus on facing the present.

articles.title: TVK confident of forming govt in Tamil Nadu, Vijay to be CM forever
articles.description: TVK Fisheries Wing Secretary Ganeshan expressed confidence in forming the govt in Tamil Nadu under actor Vijay's leadership after winning 108 seats. The party is 10 short of a majority, but Congress has extended support to the new formation.

articles.title: Dharmendra Pradhan slams TMC over killing of Suvendu Adhikari's aide
articles.description: Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan condemned the killing of Chandranath Rath, aide to Suvendu Adhikari, blaming TMC for a 'blood-soaked political culture.' He called it a 'barbaric execution' and part of a deliberate 'ecosystem of terror.'

articles.title: 'Avatar': James Cameron sued over unauthorized use of actress's face
articles.description: Actress Q'orianka Kilcher has sued James Cameron and Disney, alleging her face was used without consent at age 14 to create the 'Avatar' character Neytiri. The suit claims her likeness was extracted from a photo and used across the franchise.

articles.title: BJP slams TMC's 'Jungle Raj' after Suvendu Adhikari's PA killed
articles.description: BJP's Shehzad Poonawalla alleged political violence is in TMC's "DNA" after the killing of Suvendu Adhikari's aide. He accused TMC of creating a "Jungle Raj" in West Bengal and targeting BJP workers, citing post-poll violence.

articles.description: She was a bride for six days. Her sindoor named a military operation. One year later, Himanshi Narwal carries a grief that an entire nation borrowed, but only she owns.

articles.description: Jhalmuri is now set to be a part of the celebrations during the swearing-in ceremony of the newly elected cabinet of the BJP government in West Bengal.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari's PA murder: BJP leader says aide's killing 'pre-planned'
articles.description: West Bengal BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari has called the killing of his personal assistant a "cold-blooded and pre-planned murder." He urged party workers to maintain peace while assuring that the culprits will be brought to justice. Union Minister Sukanta Majumdar echoed these sentiments, stating that the police investigation is progressing and those responsible will not be spared.

articles.description: Vijay’s TVK shattered a 50-year-old Dravidian duopoly to emerge as the single-largest party in assembly elections earlier this week, winning 108 seats.

articles.title: Kerala Cong MLAs to discuss CM pick today
articles.description: Congress legislators in Kerala will meet Thursday to select a chief minister nominee, with intense lobbying among leaders Satheesan, Venugopal, and Chennithala.

articles.description: The stand-off warfare with Pakistan a year ago marked India’s shift from calibrated caution to calibrated coercion
